metadata.title: Why Angular Momentum Can Never Point Straight Up
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = the true/classical situation (pointing along z); CRIMSON = the quantum reality (forced off-axis, the sideways deficit)
metadata.note: Exclusions honored: no ladder-operator derivation of ell(ell+1), no Robertson-bound algebra, no spherical-harmonic formulas. Mechanism only: |L| = hbar*sqrt(ell(ell+1)) > ell*hbar = max Lz, so the vector must lean off-axis.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

source_of_truth: beat_sheet.json — edit the sheet, not this file

beats.prompt: Set it to the maximum allowed orientation. The z-component reaches its ceiling. And the vector still leans — sweeping a cone around the axis, never lining up.
